snps.effect_template: Factor V Leiden mutation. A allele causes activated protein C resistance, major VTE risk factor.
Prothrombin F2 G20210A variant increasing VTE risk through elevated prothrombin levels.
ABO blood group non-O genotype associated with increased VTE risk (OR ~1.5).
Protein C variant affecting anticoagulant function and VTE susceptibility.
Fibrinogen gamma-chain variant modulating fibrin polymerization and thrombosis risk.
Antithrombin III variant affecting anticoagulant pathway and VTE risk.
Glycoprotein VI platelet receptor variant affecting platelet activation and thrombosis risk.

recommendation_template: If carrier: inform doctors before surgery, during pregnancy, or when considering hormonal contraception. Avoid prolonged immobility. Stay hydrated during long flights.

studies.title: Mutation in blood coagulation factor V associated with resistance to activated protein C
studies.finding: Factor V Leiden (rs6025 A) confers 5x increased VTE risk (heterozygous)

studies.title: Genetic risk factors for venous thromboembolism
studies.finding: Identified multiple VTE susceptibility loci including F2, ABO, PROC, FGG, SERPINC1, and GP6 variants
